mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer demand and economic health, directly translating to higher corporate revenues and earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical level, it indicates a tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and disposable income, which in turn supports corporate profits and drives S&P 500 upward over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If, after UNRATE drops below 7.0%, the average 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over the subsequent 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of persistently low federal funds rates and stable, moderate inflation creates an environment where corporate borrowing costs are minimal and real returns on equities are attractive compared to fixed income, leading to positive S&P 500 performance over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL annual growth between 1.5% and 2.5%
failure_condition: If, under the specified conditions, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat for more than 50% of the triggered periods over any 3-year span.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, a significant acceleration in industrial production indicates increasing manufacturing output and strong business investment, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If, during confirmed economic expansion periods, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a positive correlation with INDPRO growth, or if average returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained drop in housing starts suggests a contraction in construction activity and broader economic demand, often preceding a slowdown in consumer spending and business investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines by more than 10% from its 6-month peak
failure_condition: If, after the trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, providing a continuous tailwind for S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rolling 1-year correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 5-day returns drops below zero for more than 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: In an inflationary environment, an increase in the Federal Funds Rate by the central bank to curb inflation directly raises the cost of capital for businesses and increases the discount rate for future earnings, leading to a decline in S&P 500 valuations over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3.0%
failure_condition: If, during periods where both FEDFUNDS rises and CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive (> 50% of instances).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust GDP growth and a low unemployment rate indicates a strong, healthy economy with high aggregate demand and full employment, which provides a solid foundation for corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 performance over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If, when GDPC1 annual growth exceeds 2.5% and UNRATE is below 6.0%, the average 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative or statistically insignificant (t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year period.
